your_book:
Thursday was a tale of two very different stories inside Jaleel's book. Abbott Laboratories was the standout, surging +10.71% to $2,866 — the headline cites it as one of the day's big movers, and the timing is notable given ABT's next earnings flag was literally today (Jul 16). The position is now +14.3% in the green and carries a STRONG BUY verdict, so the thesis is holding. Zoetis (+3.19%), ADBE (+4.79%), BSX (+3.67%), and EFX (+4.67%) also contributed positively. On the other side, RKLB was punished hard — down -11.61% on a day the broader chip/Nasdaq complex sold off, deepening its already painful -27.7% unrealized loss. That position sits at $1,751 with an AVOID verdict and a QARP score of just 44.7; price and thesis have been diverging for some time. AVGO also slipped -5.03%, and NVDA shed -2.4% as Korean semi sentiment weighed on the complex despite solid TSMC numbers.

The drift to watch most carefully is BSX and LULU. Both carry elevated risk flags — BSX's FY26 growth guide was slashed to 7–8.5% with flat guidance through Q3 (next print Jul 29), yet the stock bounced +3.67% today. A one-day pop against a deteriorating fundamental backdrop is not thesis confirmation. LULU is similarly underwater at -7.1% with five straight quarters of negative Americas comps and tariff margin pressure; its next catalyst isn't until Sep 2, meaning this drift has room to continue. ADBE's +4.79% day is welcome, but the -4.0% unrealized loss and the AI-disruption narrative (not the numbers) remain the real headwind — the CFO exit and ARR de-emphasis aren't resolved by a single green session. Overall the book ended with an unrealized loss of -$82.17 (-0.38%) on $21,608 in assets.

Watch closely: NOW reports Jul 22 and is the single largest weight at 11.1% — it was flat-to-down today (-0.69%) and remains the book's biggest concentration risk in any hawkish-Fed or software de-rate scenario. KO's fairlife ransomware-driven U.S. production suspension is a new wrinkle on a small position ($171), but worth monitoring for any reputational or volume fallout. Cash is essentially nil at $75, leaving Jaleel no dry powder to act on any of these moving parts.

attention.headline: AVOID-rated, -27.7% unrealized, -11.61% today — conviction test
attention.note: With an AVOID verdict, a QARP of 44.7, and the position now down more than a quarter of its cost basis, there is no data-supported reason to hold; the chip-selloff context today only amplifies the existing structural concerns.

attention.headline: Guidance slashed but stock up +3.67% today — don't mistake the bounce for recovery
attention.note: FY26 growth was cut to 7–8.5% with flat guidance through Q3, and the next earnings print is Jul 29; a single up-day against that fundamental backdrop warrants skepticism, not comfort.

attention.headline: Largest position reports in 6 days — concentration risk is live
attention.note: At 11.1% of the book and a high multiple, NOW's Jul 22 print is the single biggest near-term binary for the portfolio, and today's -0.69% drift suggests the market is already cautious heading in.

attention.headline: Fifth straight negative comps, risk flag elevated, no catalyst until Sep 2
attention.note: Price and thesis continue to diverge with -7.1% unrealized loss and no near-term event to reset the narrative; the drift is slow but persistent and deserves a position-size review.
